rag_rules: Fix unresolved property references by ensuring all property definitions and constants are properly declared in the AADL model or its dependencies before use in AGREE contracts.
Resolve type errors in AGREE expressions by ensuring all identifiers and helper declarations have valid, resolvable types matching the expected scalar types (int, real, bool).
Remove or correct AGREE clauses that reference invalid or unresolvable features, constants, or property definitions to prevent downstream validation failures.
generic_rules: When generating AGREE contracts, ensure all referenced identifiers are visible and resolvable within the same component type or implementation scope.
Use only visible AADL features or helper declarations in AGREE contracts to avoid type resolution errors.
Validate AGREE scalar type declarations against supported types (int, real, bool) to prevent resolution failures.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks within valid component type or implementation scopes.
Property references in AGREE must resolve to valid definitions in the AADL model or configured dependencies.
Helper declarations in AGREE must have explicit type annotations matching the expected scalar types.
anti_patterns: Avoid referencing property definitions or constants that are not declared in the AADL model or its dependencies.
Do not include AGREE clauses that reference invalid or unresolvable identifiers without first resolving their definitions.
irrelevant_topics: AADL package references and with clauses
Component type vs implementation scope placement
Subcomponent port usage in component type contracts
